leyten/shard.title: Verified weight-fetch deploy path (range fetch + puller) (#45)
leyten/shard.tldr: Verified weight-fetch deploy path (range fetch + puller) (#45). 3 files changed, +111/-16.
leyten/shard.plainEnglish: The scattered deploy pulls each stage's block with a raw, UNVERIFIED snapshot_download (m25_pull_range). Route it through the content-addressed manifest instead so every byte is re-hashed against a signed manifest before it touches disk — a malicious mirror or peer physically cannot feed corrupt weights (sha256 won't match, load fails closed).
leyten/shard.whyItMatters: Looks related to Protocol → Signed receipts.

leyten/c0mpute.title: chat: keep the generating-image skeleton up until the async render lands
leyten/c0mpute.tldr: chat: keep the generating-image skeleton up until the async render lands. 1 files changed, +16/-3.
leyten/c0mpute.plainEnglish: With async rendering the model's text turn (job:complete) finishes before the image, so job:complete was clearing the 'generating image…' skeleton ~1s in, then the image popped in later with no progress indicator between. Track an awaitingImageRef from job:generating_image until job:image / job:image_error; job:complete no longer clears the skeleton while a render is in flight. Reset on send and hard job error; 200s safety net.
leyten/c0mpute.whyItMatters: Looks related to Product → Frontier models in chat.

leyten/c0mpute.title: chat: render generate_image async so the worker never times out waiting
leyten/c0mpute.tldr: chat: render generate_image async so the worker never times out waiting. 5 files changed, +140/-28.
leyten/c0mpute.plainEnglish: generate_image used to block the worker's tool-result wait on the full GPU render; a cold/queued render exceeded the worker's 30s ceiling and surfaced 'Tool execution timed out'. The model never uses the rendered image (the tool result is text), so the tool now does its sync work (safety + credit spend + workflow build) and returns a PendingImage; the orchestrator sends the tool result immediately and fires the render async, delivering job:image to the user when it lands or refunding + emitting job:image_error on failure. Frontend attaches a late image to its saved assistant message by job_id (retroactive), or buffers it for job:complete if it arrives first; new job:image_error handler shows
leyten/c0mpute.whyItMatters: Looks related to Product → Bigger uncensored models.

leyten/c0mpute.title: worker: raise tool-result wait 30s->200s so image renders don't time out
leyten/c0mpute.tldr: worker: raise tool-result wait 30s->200s so image renders don't time out. 2 files changed, +8/-3.
leyten/c0mpute.plainEnglish: The worker abandoned the tool-result wait after 30s, but the orchestrator gives image renders up to 180s (IMAGE_JOB_TIMEOUT_MS) and always returns a result within that window. A cold/queued image render exceeded 30s, surfacing 'Tool execution timed out (30s)' while the image was still rendering. Raise to 200s (> the orchestrator ceiling). Bump to 2.8.3.
leyten/c0mpute.whyItMatters: Looks related to Product → Frontier models in chat.

leyten/c0mpute.title: chat: never feed generated images back to the model as input
leyten/c0mpute.tldr: chat: never feed generated images back to the model as input. 2 files changed, +16/-1.
leyten/c0mpute.plainEnglish: Generated images are display-only output (shown inline, never stored). The chat re-sent the prior generated image into the model's context on the next turn when the tier advertised vision; the max model has no vision projector, so ollama 500'd ('image input is not supported - mmproj'). Fixed both layers: the orchestrator strips assistant-message images before dispatch (covers all clients, no rebuild), and the frontend only attaches user-uploaded images to context (assistant/generated images excluded). User-uploaded images still reach vision-capable workers.
leyten/c0mpute.whyItMatters: Looks related to Protocol → Ever-bigger models.

leyten/c0mpute.title: chat: faithful uncensored image-prompt translation
leyten/c0mpute.tldr: chat: faithful uncensored image-prompt translation. 2 files changed, +3/-3.
leyten/c0mpute.plainEnglish: The generate_image tool let the model rewrite explicit user requests into tame ones (e.g. 'lady with nice tits' -> 'elegant lady in a pastel dress'), defeating the uncensored pillar. The backend already renders faithfully (NSFW gate open, only the CSAM floor enforced) — the softening was the chat model writing a sanitized prompt arg against a neutral system prompt. Now the tool description tells it to translate the request literally without sanitizing/adding coverage, and the native system prompt states the platform is uncensored and not to water down lawful requests. CSAM hard-line still blocks regardless.
leyten/c0mpute.whyItMatters: Looks related to Protocol → Signed receipts.

leyten/c0mpute.title: free-subsidy: reserve worst-case worker payout at submit so the cap can't strand workers
leyten/c0mpute.tldr: free-subsidy: reserve worst-case worker payout at submit so the cap can't strand workers. 1 files changed, +23/-7.
leyten/c0mpute.plainEnglish: The submit gate used a bare 'paid subsidy >= cap' check while payout needs 'paid + thisJob <= cap'. Since the paid total only rises when a worker is actually paid, once spend stalled one job under the cap, submit kept admitting free prompts that payout then refused to fund — every worker serving a free job past that point earned $0 until the UTC reset (stranded 71 workers / 350 jobs on Jun 20). Now both the anon and onboarding free-prompt paths reserve the worst-case worker share at submit and reject when the daily/hourly cap can't cover it, so any admitted free job is guaranteed payable. The onboarding path keeps the user's free-prompt count instead of burning it.
